acceptance_targets: [C09-DIALOGUE-001] PASS if, in revised lines 332-381, Nora has at least three lines that are clearly attributable to her (museum-operations specifics + pragmatic empathy, not generic legal boilerplate), while Daniel's revised lines 388-420 remain explicitly counsel-formal. FAIL if either speaker's lines can be swapped with minimal mismatch.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if, in revised lines 332-381, Nora has at least three lines that are clearly attributable to her (museum-operations specifics + pragmatic empathy, not generic legal boilerplate), while Daniel's revised lines 388-420 remain explicitly counsel-formal. FAIL if either speaker's lines can be swapped with minimal mismatch.
findings.evidence: Nora and outside counsel converge into near-identical legal cadence: snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:356; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:376; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:388; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:394; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:410.
findings.problem: Idiolect separation weakens in the backlash sequence. Nora (fatigued, pragmatic institutional voice) and Daniel (outside-counsel boilerplate) read too close in register, syntax pressure, and legal noun stacking, flattening character distinctness under scene pressure.
findings.rewrite_direction: Differentiate calls in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:332-381 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:388-420. Keep Daniel formally legal; shift Nora toward her style-bible profile with tighter operational specifics, controlled empathy, and more spoken-texture contractions while retaining authority.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Focus on dialogue realism and voice separation: idiolect fidelity, leverage shifts, pressure cadence, anti-transcript behavior, natural colloquial texture (contractions/interruptions/slang when character-true), and pressure-true profanity usage per aesthetic risk policy.
revision_pass.label: dialogue/idiolect/cadence fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: When choosing between reputationally safe abstraction and specific discomfort, choose specificity. Render addiction, sexuality, deceit, class shame, and grief at lived scale. In edge cases, preserve character truth and causal accountability over reader comfort or institutional decorum.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene demands bodily, moral, or economic specificity, euphemistic phrasing is treated as a failed draft and must be rewritten with concrete stakes and observable consequence.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: high - contractions are the spoken default; full forms appear only for emphasis, ritual language, or character-specific formal posture

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate scene density and pressure modes: pair high-conflict chapters with quiet domestic or documentary chapters, vary dialogue-to-interiority ratios, and prevent identical chapter skeletons in consecutive sequence.
prose_style_profile.diction: contemporary literary diction with class-aware, borough-specific concreteness; avoid prestige-neutral workshop language
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: close third centered on Marisol, with rare brief distance expansions for institutional scenes
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: alternating tensile compression and reflective extension; line-level cadence should carry emotional pressure, not just information
